title: thymus serpyllum f. kosteleckyanus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. kosteleckyanus, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a fragrant, low-growing herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum f. kosteleckyanus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and relieve respiratory problems.
